# Success

<!--
Projects should have a definition of done that is measurable, and a
thorough understanding going in of what the risks are to delivery
-->

## Definition of Done

Success is defined by five outcomes: code merged and released to CRAN; `partitions_iter` compliant with the `iterators` S3 interface; tested correctness and interoperability; user documentation with worked examples; and iterator-related issues resolved or explicitly triaged.

## Measuring Success

We will track the following headline metrics throughout the project:

| Metric | Success Criterion | Measurement Method |
|---|---|---|
| **Coverage** | >= 90% of new iterator code tested | `covr` report |
| **Checks** | `devtools::check()` and `R CMD check` pass cleanly | CI and CRAN dry-run |
| **Interop** | `foreach()` and `itertools` examples run without error | Tests and vignette examples |
| **Memory** | Peak memory of lazy iteration is <= 10% of equivalent bulk generation for large n | Benchmark scripts |
